short_summary: Xi in Pyongyang deepens hostile axis
rationale: Xi Jinping's confirmed visit to Pyongyang represents a qualitative step in consolidating the China-Russia-North Korea axis, directly threatening Ukraine's long-term military supply and diplomatic isolation prospects.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: Xi's confirmed visit deepens the authoritarian bloc that backs Russia with weapons, technology and diplomatic cover. For Ukraine, this means the hostile coalition supporting Russia is becoming more institutionalised, threatening long-term war sustainability.

top_events.title: Vance says Israeli PM Netanyahu 'has got some things wrong'
top_events.summary: US political attention and bandwidth remains split between Middle East and Ukraine; any US-Israel friction could indirectly affect the pace of Western support decisions for Ukraine.

top_events.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes
top_events.summary: Global commodity and energy price pressures from El Niño could strain Western economies and indirectly reduce fiscal space for Ukraine aid, but effect is distant and indirect.
